competencies: Plan and execute an engineering project in a goal-directed way
Establish the contacts and resources required for project work
Document project results in a written report
Present and defend project outcomes
description_en: An individual or team project in which students independently plan, execute, document, and defend an engineering project linked to institute research or teaching.

competencies: Implement data-driven engineering activities for production systems
Write object-oriented software in Python
Apply graph theory and graph-based optimization methods
Model production systems using PAN modelling
description_en: A blended intensive course teaching Python-based object-oriented software engineering and graph-based optimization for data-driven production system engineering.

competencies: Understand microstructure-property relations of emerging materials
Interpret complex phase diagrams of metallic and high-temperature materials
Apply materials analysis methods such as XRD, DFT, and APT
Assess innovative processing and mechanical testing techniques for materials selection
description_en: A lecture series on recent materials science developments, covering new materials, microstructure-property relations, processing, testing, and analytical methods.

competencies: Apply computer-aided design methods to product design tasks
Use industrial-design-typical design tools and methods
Manage interfaces between CAID, CAD, and ergonomic software systems
Create complex visualizations of products
description_en: A practice-oriented course on computer-aided industrial design, focusing on CAID/CAD tools, software interfaces, and complex product visualization.

competencies: Assess resource availability, flow, and supply conflicts
Describe recycling processes and their energy demand
Evaluate sustainable and efficient use of resources
Reintegrate recycled materials into manufacturing processes
description_en: A course on resource availability, sustainability, and recycling technologies, enabling students to describe recycling processes and return recycled materials to production.

competencies: Understand the interaction of material, design, and loading for safe component design
Detect, analyze, and prevent damage in metallic components
Identify fracture and corrosion damage types and topographies
Apply standards and regulations in failure detection
description_en: A course on failure analysis of metallic components, covering damage mechanisms, fracture and corrosion types, and damage-preventing component design.

competencies: Select appropriate welding processes for a given application
Design quality-oriented welding technologies
Apply arc, laser, electron-beam, and pressure welding processes
Implement mechanization, automation, and quality assurance of welding
description_en: An advanced course on welding manufacturing processes, teaching process selection, quality-oriented welding technology, and automation of welded joint production.

competencies: Design weld-appropriate and requirement-driven welded constructions
Dimension welded joints and provide conformity proof
Assess strength and crack avoidance in high-strength welded joints
Apply standards, codes, and IIW recommendations to welded structures
description_en: A course on the design of welded structures, covering weld-appropriate detailing, joint dimensioning, fatigue strength, and relevant standards and codes.

competencies: Apply 1D and 3D simulation tools in powertrain development
Design and model engine concepts including turbocharging and injection systems
Model combustion, mixture formation, and pollutant formation
Simulate full vehicle systems and fuel-cell drive systems
description_en: A course on simulating internal combustion engine processes using 1D and 3D-CFD tools, covering engine design, combustion, and full powertrain modelling.

competencies: Model linear and non-linear dynamic systems
Apply time integration and eigenvalue analysis methods
Set up rigid and flexible multibody dynamic models
Evaluate and analyze results of numerical dynamic simulations
description_en: A course on simulation methods for dynamical systems, covering vibration dynamics, time integration, eigenvalue analysis, multibody systems, and nonlinearities.

competencies: Describe how gravity affects cellular systems and the human organism
Evaluate R&D platforms and constraints for space research
Analyze the space economy and low-Earth-orbit applications
Understand startup creation and commercial spaceflight in the DACHL region
description_en: A cross-disciplinary course introducing space biotechnology, gravitational biology, human spaceflight, and the commercial space economy in low Earth orbit.

competencies: Understand the generation and use of laser and electron beams for manufacturing
Apply beam processes for welding, cutting, drilling, and marking
Assess material suitability for beam welding
Design beam-welded joints and ensure their quality
description_en: A course on laser and electron-beam technology as manufacturing tools for joining and cutting, covering beam systems, material effects, and joint quality.

competencies: Transfer structural-dynamic systems into mechanical substitute models
Solve vibration differential equations for single and multi-degree-of-freedom systems
Derive load spectra using classification methods
Estimate component service life via damage accumulation models
description_en: A course on structural dynamics and durability analysis, covering vibration modelling, modal analysis, load spectra derivation, and service-life estimation.

competencies: Classify ERP functionality within a company's information architecture
Understand the services and functionality of ERP systems
Master core ERP processes and their application limits
Apply production control methods in series and variant manufacturing
description_en: A practice-oriented course on enterprise resource planning systems, covering ERP functionality, business processes, and production control in series manufacturing.

competencies: Analyze requirements and challenges in logistics networks
Optimize logistics networks holistically and scenario-based
Apply supply chain design, planning, execution, and controlling
Conduct data acquisition, SWOT analysis, and scenario evaluation
description_en: A course on the design and optimization of logistics and supply networks, covering supply chain planning, scenario evaluation, and the logistics service market.

competencies: Analyze requirements and limits of logistics networks
Optimize logistics networks holistically and scenario-based
Apply supply chain design, planning, execution, and controlling
Evaluate the role of logistics service providers as network designers
description_en: A course on logistics networks and logistics service providers, covering supply chain design, scenario-based network optimization, and inventory management.

competencies: Apply aesthetic design means of form, colour, and material for sustainability
Integrate sustainable design into integrated product development
Analyze sustainability potentials of object and usage aesthetics
Investigate perception and usage effects of aesthetic design choices
description_en: A course on sustainable product design through aesthetic means of form, colour, and material within integrated product development processes.

competencies: Apply engineering methods for production and control system design
Engineer mechatronic units for production systems
Apply object-oriented methods and VDI guidelines to production engineering
Model production systems using UML, SysML, and the RAMI toolbox
description_en: A course on systems engineering for manufacturing systems, covering mechatronic design, VDI guidelines, object orientation, and SysML/RAMI modelling.

competencies: Plan and control innovation processes in industrial organizations
Initiate product, technology, and process innovation decisions
Classify innovations and align innovation processes strategically
Evaluate the success and risk of innovations
description_en: A course on technical innovation management, enabling students to plan, classify, steer, and evaluate product, process, and technology innovation processes.

competencies: Assess possibilities and limits of joining, coating, and assembly technologies
Select application-optimized processes and technologies
Apply arc, electron-beam, laser, and hybrid joining technologies
Use thermal spraying and innovative coating processes
description_en: A course on technologies for joining, coating, and assembly, covering welding, thermal spraying, additive joining, and application-optimized process selection.

competencies: Name and describe primary shaping, forming, and machining processes
Assess possibilities and limits of these manufacturing technologies
Select manufacturing processes for exemplary applications
Apply casting, powder-metallurgical, additive, forming, and cutting methods
description_en: A course on technologies for primary shaping, forming, and machining, covering casting, powder metallurgy, additive, forming, and cutting processes.

competencies: Analyze and identify friction and wear problems and their mechanisms
Optimize tribologically loaded components and tribosystems
Assess conditions for energy-efficient and sustainable tribosystem design
Use software and testing equipment to evaluate tribosystems
description_en: A course on tribological product optimization, covering friction, wear, lubrication, coatings, and energy-efficient design of tribosystems.

competencies: Apply strategic corporate management and planning methods
Analyze the strategic environment using SWOT, PESTEL, and scenario techniques
Develop existing and new business models
Create a detailed business plan from idea to implementation
description_en: A course on strategic business planning and corporate management, covering strategy analysis, business model development, and Industry 4.0 project implementation.

competencies: Understand the fundamentals of internal combustion engine design
Analyze thermodynamics and working processes of reciprocating engines
Compare spark-ignition and diesel engines and their combustion
Assess exhaust emissions, aftertreatment, and future engine trends
description_en: A course on internal combustion engines, covering reciprocating machine fundamentals, thermodynamics, combustion, emissions, and future powertrain developments.

competencies: Discuss gearing problems for involute spur gears using technical terminology
Understand the gear manufacturing process from roughing to finishing
Select and apply measuring devices for gear inspection
Interpret gear measurement and deviation results
description_en: A course on gear technology, covering the geometry, manufacturing, and metrological inspection of involute spur gears and gear pairs.

competencies: Explain sound radiation and transmission of vibrating structures
Apply fundamentals of technical acoustics and wave propagation in solids
Measure vibro-acoustic phenomena experimentally
Apply methods to actively reduce radiated noise
description_en: A course with lab exercises on vibro-acoustics, covering sound radiation and transmission of structures, measurement methods, and active noise reduction.

competencies: Understand and apply the fundamentals of heat treatment
Distinguish heat treatment processes and their application areas
Describe material microstructures and properties after heat treatment
Recognize and avoid defects and distortions during heat treatment
description_en: A course on thermal treatment of materials, covering hardening processes, phase transformations, microstructure-property effects, and defect avoidance.

competencies: Analyze requirements of hydrogen as an alternative fuel
Assess sustainability potential and safety standards for hydrogen
Evaluate hydrogen production, storage, distribution, and infrastructure
Understand hydrogen combustion engines and fuel-cell drive systems
description_en: A course on hydrogen technology and hydrogen drives, covering production, storage, safety, life-cycle assessment, combustion engines, and fuel-cell systems.

competencies: Assess the weldability of ferrous and non-ferrous metals
Understand material changes in the heat-affected zone and weld metal
Select weld fillers and auxiliary materials for different materials
Determine pre- and post-weld heat treatment measures
description_en: A course on materials and welding, covering the weldability of metals, heat-affected zone changes, weld fillers, and required heat treatments.

competencies: Survey materials and their processing for automotive applications
Use material characteristics for material selection
Explain manufacturing possibilities and limits for automotive materials
Assess lightweight, safety, and e-mobility material concepts
description_en: A course on materials and processes in automotive production, covering steels, alloys, composites, lightweight concepts, and forming and joining technologies.

competencies: Link forming, casting, and thermal processes to material properties
Understand metal synthesis via melting, casting, and powder metallurgy
Analyze microstructure-changing processes and phase transformations
Relate synthesis processes to properties of ceramics, polymers, and composites
description_en: A course on materials processing technology, linking primary shaping, forming, and thermal treatment to the microstructural and mechanical properties of materials.

competencies: Name and describe machine tool programming methods for machining
Explain and assess specifics of programming for machining processes
Design simple machine tool programs
Apply computer-based controls and manual and automated programming
description_en: A course on machine tool programming for machining processes, covering computer-based controls and manual and automated program creation.

competencies: Determine time data efficiently for industrial time management
Optimize production systems from a time-economy perspective
Analyze and synthesize work sequences using time-structuring schemes
Apply time-study methods such as predetermined motion time systems
description_en: A course on time management and data collection, teaching efficient time-data acquisition and time-economy optimization of production systems.
